# host_aur_helper (Resource)

Bootstraps and manages an AUR helper package without requiring an existing AUR helper.

This resource bootstraps `yay` or `paru` directly from its AUR Git repository
with `makepkg`; it does not require an existing AUR helper. Declare Pacman's
`base-devel` and `git` packages as dependencies so they are installed before the
helper is built. AUR package resources should in turn depend on this resource.

The provider runs `makepkg` as the unprivileged Terraform process user. Run
Terraform as the configured target user rather than as root. Before the build,
the provider validates sudo authentication once and supplies `makepkg` with a
controlled configuration that invokes Pacman through the canonical `sudo -n`
path. A long build can still fail if the cached sudo credential expires; rerun
after `sudo -v` rather than running the build as root.

> **Security:** AUR repositories are user-contributed and their `PKGBUILD`
> files execute code as the Terraform process user. This resource clones the
> current, mutable AUR Git HEAD. Review and trust the selected package before
> applying it; Terraform does not audit or sandbox the build.

The resolved `yay` or `paru` executable must be owned by the configured Pacman
package. The provider verifies that ownership before adopting, using, or
removing the helper package. Concurrent resources requesting the same helper
share one bootstrap operation, and the installed package is kept with Pacman's
explicit install reason.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_package_pacman" "base_devel" {
  name = "base-devel"
}

resource "host_package_pacman" "git" {
  name = "git"
}

# Review and trust yay's mutable AUR PKGBUILD before allowing Terraform to
# build it as the target user.
resource "host_aur_helper" "yay" {
  name = "yay"

  depends_on = [
    host_package_pacman.base_devel,
    host_package_pacman.git,
  ]
}

resource "host_package_aur" "wl_kbptr" {
  name = "wl-kbptr"

  depends_on = [
    host_aur_helper.yay,
  ]
}
```

## Import

Import a helper whose executable and package have the same name:

```shell
terraform import host_aur_helper.yay yay
```

For a package variant, use `<name>:<package>`:

```shell
terraform import host_aur_helper.yay yay:yay-bin
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) AUR helper executable name. Supported values are `yay` and `paru`.

### Optional

- `delete_on_destroy` (Boolean) Remove the helper package when this resource is destroyed. Defaults to false so provider bootstrap tooling is retained.
- `install_reason` (String) Desired and observed Pacman install reason. The only supported desired value is `explicit`; refresh records `dependency` after external drift so the next apply restores `explicit`.
- `package` (String) AUR package that provides the helper executable. Defaults to `name`; variants such as `yay-bin` are supported.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier, equal to `name`.
- `installed_version` (String) Installed helper package version.
- `path` (String) Resolved helper executable path.
